title: Polish, Documentation, and Testing
impact: Medium - Completes TaskScheduler integration, adds documentation, and ensures comprehensive test coverage

graphTraversal.target: findAllPaths supports AbortSignal cancellation and maxPaths limit

streamingExporter.current: No progress reporting during streaming exports

testCoverage.target: Unit tests for operationUtils + integration tests for all managers

tasks.title: Add Cancellation Support to GraphTraversal.findAllPaths()
tasks.description: Add AbortSignal cancellation support and optional maxPaths limit to GraphTraversal.findAllPaths() method for early termination of path-finding in dense graphs.

implementation.purpose: Enable cancellation of path-finding operations that may take a long time in densely connected graphs.
keyDecisions: Add signal and maxPaths to TraversalOptions interface
Check cancellation at each DFS recursion level
Support early termination when maxPaths reached
Keep original return type PathResult[] (NOT CancellableResult wrapper)

currentCode.signature: async findAllPaths(source: string, target: string, maxDepth: number = 5, options: TraversalOptions = {}): Promise<PathResult[]>

stepByStep.details: Add signal?: AbortSignal and maxPaths?: number fields
Import from utils/index.js

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check in dfsAllPaths helper function
stepByStep.details: At start of recursion: checkCancellation(opts.signal, 'findAllPaths')
If paths.length >= maxPaths, return early from DFS
Add cancellation check in BFS loop every 100 iterations

stepByStep.action: Add tests for cancellation and maxPaths
stepByStep.details: Test cancellation throws OperationCancelledError, test maxPaths limits results

acceptanceCriteria: TraversalOptions includes signal and maxPaths fields
findAllPaths checks cancellation at each recursion
All existing traversal tests pass (backward compatible)

tasks.title: Add Progress Callback to StreamingExporter
tasks.description: Add progress callback support to StreamingExporter.streamJSONL() and streamCSV() methods for tracking progress during large streaming exports.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during streaming exports, showing entity/relation export progress.
keyDecisions: Create StreamingExportOptions interface with onProgress?: ProgressCallback

stepByStep.details: interface StreamingExportOptions { onProgress?: ProgressCallback; signal?: AbortSignal; }
async streamJSONL(graph, options?: StreamingExportOptions): Promise<StreamResult>

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting in entity loop
stepByStep.details: Every 100 entities, report progress with percentage and bytes written

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting in relation loop
stepByStep.details: Every 100 relations, report progress
Same progress pattern for CSV export
Test progress callback receives expected updates

acceptanceCriteria: Progress callback receives updates every 100 items
All existing streaming tests pass (backward compatible)

tasks.title: Create TaskScheduler Integration Guide
tasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation for the TaskScheduler utilities and the new progress/cancellation patterns used across all managers.

implementation.purpose: Provide comprehensive documentation for TaskScheduler utilities and async operation patterns.
keyDecisions: Include overview of all TaskScheduler utilities
Document the existing ProgressCallback type (object-based format)
Provide examples for each manager's progress/cancellation features
Include performance considerations and best practices

stepByStep.details: Create file with comprehensive structure
Explain TaskScheduler purpose, available utilities (batchProcess, withRetry, debounce, throttle, TaskQueue)
Document object-based format: { completed, total, percentage, currentTaskId? }
Explain onProgress, signal, and priority fields
Document each manager's progress/cancellation capabilities with examples
Guidelines for when to use, progress patterns, cancellation handling, error handling
Document overhead, batch sizes, throttling
Add reference to new guide in Documentation section

acceptanceCriteria: All core utilities documented with examples
All manager integrations documented with code examples
Best practices section provides actionable guidance
CLAUDE.md updated to reference the new guide

tasks.title: Add Unit and Integration Tests for Operation Utilities
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for the operationUtils module and integration tests that verify progress/cancellation across multiple managers in realistic scenarios.

implementation.purpose: Ensure operationUtils functions work correctly and TaskScheduler integration works across all managers.
keyDecisions: Create unit tests for checkCancellation, createProgressReporter, createProgress, executeWithPhases
Create integration tests for EntityManager, CompressionManager progress/cancellation
Test combined workflows with progress tracking
Test performance overhead of progress callbacks < 20%

stepByStep.details: Unit tests for all operationUtils functions
Test with undefined signal, non-aborted signal, aborted signal, operation name in error
Test returns undefined for no callback, throttling behavior, always reports 0% and 100%
Test correct values, zero total handling, currentTaskId inclusion
Test phase ordering, progress reporting, cancellation
Integration tests for multi-manager scenarios
Create 500 entities with progress tracking, abort mid-operation
Import -> compress -> export pipeline with progress
Verify progress callback overhead < 20% compared to no callback

acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests exist for all operationUtils functions
Combined workflow test verifies end-to-end progress
All tests pass with npm test

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
Integration tests cover all major managers
npm test passes with all new tests
notes: Sprint 3 depends on both Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 being complete
Documentation should be comprehensive enough for new developers
Integration tests should use realistic data sizes (100-1000 items)
Performance tests should account for CI environment variability (use 20% overhead threshold)
Use the existing ProgressCallback type from taskScheduler.ts in all examples
